title: Slurries of inorganic materials for forming solid structures
abstract: The present invention relates to a slurry comprising i) an inorganic material, ii) a binder, and iii) an organic solvent of formula (I), wherein R 1 is H, d-Ce-Alkyl, R 2 , R 3 , R 4 , R 5 , R 6 , R 7 , R 8 , R 9 , R 10 , R 11 are independently from each other either H or Me, and n is O oM. The present invention further relates to the use of such slurries as a component for producing a solid structure, to a process for preparing a solid structure and to a process for preparing a cathode for a rechargeable electrochemical cell.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A slurry comprising an inorganic material, a binder, and an organic solvent of formula (I), wherein R is H, d-Ce-Alkyl, R 2 , R 3 , R 4 , R 5 , R 6 , R 7 , R 8 , R 9 , R 10 , R 11 are independently from each other either H or Me, and n is 0 or 1 . The slurry according to claim 1 , wherein the inorganic material is an electroactive chalcogen-containing material. The slurry according to claim 2, wherein the electroactive chalcogen-containing material is selected from the group consisting of lithium-containing transition metal oxides and lithium-containing transition metal phosphates. The slurry according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the binder comprises a fluoropol- ymer. 5. The slurry according to claim 4, wherein the fluoropolymer comprises -CH2-CF2- units. The slurry according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the organic solvent of formula (I) is N-formylpyrrolidine. The slurry according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the slurry further comprises a conductive material as component (iv). 8. The slurry according to claim 7, wherein the conductive material is solid material that shows electronic and/or ionic conductivity. 9. The slurry according to claim 7, wherein the conductive material is carbon in a conduc- tive polymorph. 10. The slurry according to any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the slurry comprises i) 20 to 90 wt.-% of the inorganic material, ii) 0.1 to 10 wt.-% of the binder, iii) 9 to 79.9 wt.-% of the organic solvent of formula (I), and iv) 0.0 to 30 wt.-% of a conductive material. 1 1 . The slurry according to any of claims 7 to 10, wherein the inorganic material is an elec- troactive chalcogen-containing material, the binder is a fluoropolymer, the organic solvent of formula (I) is N-formylpyrrolidine and the conductive material is carbon in a conductive polymorph. 12. The slurry according to claim 1 1 , wherein the slurry comprises i) 40 to 80 wt.-% of the electroactive chalcogen-containing material, ii) 0.1 to 5 wt.-% of the fluoropolymer, iii) 19 to 59.8 wt.-% of N-formylpyrrolidine, and iv) 0.1 to 5 wt.-% of carbon in a conductive polymorph. 13. Use of the slurry according to any of claims 1 to 12 as a component for producing a solid structure. 14. A process for preparing a solid structure, comprising the process steps of a) forming a body by depositing of the slurry according to any of claims 1 to 12 on or in a substrate, and b) removing the organic solvent of formula (I), by drying the body formed in process step a) at a temperature in the range from 0 Â°C to 200 Â°C. 15. A process for preparing a cathode for a rechargeable electrochemical cell, wherein the cathode comprises, v) a layer, which comprises an electroactive chalcogen-containing material, a binder and optionally a conductive material, comprising the process steps of 

 a) forming a layer by depositing of the slurry according to any of claims 1 to 12 on a substrate, and b) removing the organic solvent of formula (I), by drying the layer formed in process step a) at a temperature in the range from 0 Â°C to 200 Â°C.
